units.title: All about me
units.subtitle: Say hello, share where you live, and introduce the people around you.
units.grammar_focus: Verb 'be' — am / is / are: affirmative, negative, questions, short answers. Plus 'be called' for names.

spiral_meta.grammar_anticipation: have got — 2-3 passive exposures in passages (Unit 3 will teach explicitly)

lessons.title: Saying hello
lessons.topic: Greetings, your name, age, where you live; verb 'be' affirmative + 'be called'

steps.instruction: Ray says hello to the class. Watch and answer.

questions.question_text: How does Ray greet the class?
What does Ray want to know first?
questions.hint: Basic info you share when you meet someone.

items.definition: A friendly word you use to greet someone.
A short, friendly way to say hello.
How many years old someone is.
To have your home in a place.
items.example: I live in Ho Chi Minh City.

items.definition: The place where you live, with a street and number.
Where or when your life started.

games.instruction: Match each picture with the right word.

items.hint: You say this to greet someone.
When and where your life started.
The number and street where you live.

steps.title: Meet Linh
steps.text: Hello! My name is Linh. I am nine years old. I am a girl from Vietnam. I live in Ho Chi Minh City. My address is 14 Le Loi Street. I was born in 2016. I am in class 3A. My mum and dad are at home. I have got one brother. My brother is called Minh. He is seven. My best friend is Mai. We are happy and we love our school. Are you in our class on the internet, too?
steps.scene_description: A cheerful 9-year-old Vietnamese girl in a school uniform waving at the camera, with a name tag that says 'Linh' and a small map of Vietnam in the background.

steps.rule_pattern: Verb 'be' — I am, you are, he/she/it is. Plus 'be called' for names.
steps.explanation: We use the verb 'be' to say who we are, our age and where we live. With 'I' we use 'am'. With 'he', 'she' and 'it' we use 'is'. We will add 'are' for groups in Lesson 2. For names, we can also say 'I am called…' or 'My brother is called…'.
examples: I am Linh. I am nine.
My best friend is called Mai.
I have got a brother. (We learn 'have got' more in Unit 3.)

steps.instruction: Drag the words to build the sentence.

steps.audio_text: Ray: Hi everyone! I am Ray. I am your English teacher. Today we meet a new friend. Linh: Hello, Ray! My name is Linh. I am nine years old. I am from Vietnam. I live in Ho Chi Minh City. I am in class 3A. My best friend is called Mai. She is nine, too. Ray: Lovely! And Linh, you have got a little brother, yes? Linh: Yes. My brother is called Minh. He is seven.

questions.question: Who is the speaker at the start?
How old is Linh's best friend?

steps.prompt: Say hello to Ray! Tell him your name, age, and where you live.
steps.expected_text: Hello Ray! I am [name]. I am [age] years old. I live in [city]. My best friend is called [name].
prompts: Say your name. ('I am ___' or 'My name is ___')
Say your age. ('I am ___ years old.')
Say where you live. ('I live in ___.')
Say a best friend's name using 'be called'. ('My best friend is called ___.')

questions.question: What is a friendly way to say 'hello'?
Linh ___ in Ho Chi Minh City.
Build: brother / Minh / is / My / called

lessons.title: Where I'm from
lessons.topic: Countries, cities, towns, nationalities; verb 'be' plural and negative

steps.instruction: Ray shows the class a world map. Watch and answer.

questions.question_text: Which word means 'a small place to live, smaller than a city'?
questions.hint: A few houses together in the countryside.

items.definition: A place like Vietnam or Brazil, with its own people and government.
A big place with many people, shops and buildings.
items.example: Ho Chi Minh City is a big city.

items.definition: A place to live — smaller than a city but bigger than a village.
items.example: My grandparents live in a small town.

items.definition: A small place in the countryside with only a few houses.
items.example: Tom's aunt lives in a quiet village.
Our new classmate Wei is Chinese.

games.instruction: 60 seconds! Tap the right flag for each nationality.
Flip the cards. Match each country with its nationality.

steps.title: Our online class
steps.text: We are class 3A. We are friends. Linh and Mai are Vietnamese. They are from Ho Chi Minh City. Tom is American. He is from a small town near New York. His mum is a daughter of a Vietnamese family, so Tom speaks a little Vietnamese, too. Sophie is British. She is from London. Lucas is Brazilian. He lives in a village near the sea. We aren't in the same country, but we are in the same class on the internet. Ray, our teacher, is from the UK. He is funny and kind.
steps.scene_description: A bright illustration of an online classroom with six small kid avatars in a grid — each next to a tiny flag for Vietnam (×2), USA, UK, Brazil and a teacher in the centre with a UK flag.

questions.question: Are the friends in the same country?

steps.rule_pattern: Verb 'be' — plural (we are / they are) and negative (am not, isn't, aren't)
steps.explanation: With 'you', 'we' and 'they' we use 'are'. To make 'be' negative we add 'not'. Short forms: I'm not, he isn't, she isn't, we aren't, they aren't.
examples: I am not American. I am Vietnamese.
Sophie isn't from Brazil. She is from London.
Tom and his brother aren't in our city.
They have got friends in many countries. (Unit 3 will teach 'have got' fully.)

games.instruction: One word is wrong. Tap it.

steps.instruction: Listen and tap the sentence you hear.

steps.audio_text: Ray: Class, look at the map. Linh, where are you from? Linh: I am from Vietnam. I live in Ho Chi Minh City. Ray: And Tom? Tom: I'm American. I'm not from the same town as Linh, of course. I am from a small town near New York. Ray: Sophie? Sophie: I am British. I'm from London. Ray: Lucas, what about you? Lucas: I am Brazilian. I live in a village near the sea. My mum is a daughter of a fisherman, so the sea is everything for our family. We aren't far from a big city, but my village is very small.

questions.question: Is Tom from a big city?

steps.prompt: Tell Ray about your country and city. Say one thing you ARE and one thing you AREN'T.
steps.expected_text: I am [nationality]. I am from [country]. I live in [city / town / village]. I am not [something], I am [something].
prompts: Say your city, town or village. ('I live in ___.')
Say one thing you are NOT. ('I am not ___.')

questions.question: Choose: She ___ from Vietnam. (She is American.)
Lucas is from Brazil. He is ___.
A small place in the countryside is a ___.

lessons.title: People around me
lessons.topic: Aunt, uncle, parent, grown-up; 'be' questions and short answers

steps.instruction: Linh shows Ray a photo of her family. Watch and answer.

questions.question_text: Who is in the photo with Linh?

questions.hint: It's someone older than 18, like your mum or dad.

items.example: Five grown-ups are in the room.

items.definition: A word we use to say which place is your home country or city.
To use your voice to tell someone words.

games.instruction: Listen to the word, then type it.
Flip the card. Say the word, then check.

steps.title: Linh's people
steps.text: Hello again. Today I tell you about my family. My parents are Hoa and Tuan. My mum, Hoa, is a doctor. My dad, Tuan, is a teacher. He is not my English teacher — Ray is! My mum has got one sister. She is my aunt. Her name is Lan. She is a grown-up but she is funny and kind. My dad has got one brother. He is my uncle. His name is Quan. My grandparents live in a small town near a river. I love my family. Are you a son or a daughter, too? Ask your friend!
steps.scene_description: A warm family photo: a Vietnamese girl in the centre, two parents behind her (mum in a doctor's coat, dad with a book), and a smiling aunt and uncle on each side. A small inset shows grandparents in a town setting.

questions.question: Is Linh's dad her English teacher?
Where do Linh's grandparents live?
questions.answer: in a small town near a river
options: in a small town near a river
in a village near the sea
questions.correct_answer: in a small town near a river

steps.explanation: To ask a 'be' question we put am / is / are before the person. Short answers use only 'be' (or 'be' + not). Don't repeat the rest of the sentence.
examples: Are you Linh? — Yes, I am.
Is he your uncle? — Yes, he is.
Is she from Brazil? — No, she isn't.
Are they your grandparents? — Yes, they are.
Am I in your class? — Yes, you are.
Has she got a brother? — Yes, she has. (Unit 3 will explain 'have got' questions fully.)

games.instruction: Fill the gap with Am, Is or Are.

steps.instruction: Change the sentence into a question.

steps.audio_text: Ray: Hi Linh! Is this a photo of your family? Linh: Yes, it is. Ray: Are the two people behind you your parents? Linh: Yes, they are. My mum is a doctor and my dad is a teacher. Ray: Is the woman on the left your aunt? Linh: Yes, she is. Her name is Lan. Ray: Is the man on the right your uncle? Linh: Yes, he is. He is called Quan. He has got two sons — they are my cousins. Ray: And the grandparents? Are they in the photo? Linh: No, they aren't. They live in a small town and they are not on the internet today.

questions.question: Is the woman on the left Linh's aunt?
Are the grandparents in the photo?

steps.prompt: Show Ray three people in your world. For each one, say who they are and use 'be' questions Ray can answer.
steps.expected_text: This is [name]. Is he / she my [aunt / uncle / parent / friend]? Yes, he / she is. He / She is [age] years old.
prompts: Ask a 'be' question Ray can answer. ('Is he my ___?')
Repeat for a second and a third person.
End with one age sentence using 'is'. ('He / She is ___ years old.')

questions.question: Your mother and father are your ___.
True or false: 'Is she British?' — 'No, she isn't.' is correct.

lessons.title: All about me — Review
lessons.topic: Cumulative review: hello, name, age, address, country, nationality, family; verb 'be' (all forms) + 'be called'

steps.instruction: It's review time! Ray asks you to remember the unit. Answer 2 quick questions.

questions.question_text: What did we learn in this unit?
questions.correct_answer: all about me, my country, my family
options: all about me, my country, my family

questions.question_text: Which verb did we practise the most?
questions.hint: It has am, is and are.

games.instruction: Climb the ladder! Choose the word that fits each rung.

items.prompt: Say hi to someone you meet:
Linh is from Vietnam. She is ___:
A very small place in the countryside is a ___:
Mum and dad together are your ___:
How many years old you are is your ___:

games.instruction: 90 seconds! Tap the right picture for each word.

steps.title: Linh's whole world
steps.text: Hello! Let me tell you all about me again. My name is Linh and I am nine years old. I was born in Vietnam. My address is 14 Le Loi Street, Ho Chi Minh City. I am Vietnamese, but my class is from many countries. Tom is American. He is from a small town near New York. Sophie is British and lives in London. Lucas is Brazilian. He lives in a quiet village near the sea. My parents are Hoa and Tuan. My mum is a doctor and my dad is a teacher. I have got one brother. He is called Minh and he is seven. My aunt is called Lan and my uncle is called Quan. My grandparents live in a small town near a river. They are not on the internet today, but they say hello to me on the phone. We aren't in the same city, but we are a happy family. Are we lucky? Yes, we are!
steps.scene_description: A wide, cosy scene of Linh in the centre with small portraits of her family, friends and online classmates connected by friendly lines and small flags. A river and a small town are drawn behind the grandparents.

questions.question: Who lives in a small town near a river?
Is Linh's class from one country?
What is the name of Linh's aunt?

patterns: be: Am I…? / Is he/she/it…? / Are you/we/they…? (questions)
Short answers: Yes, I am. / No, she isn't. / Yes, they are. / No, we aren't.
Be called + name: My brother is called Minh.

games.instruction: Read the sentence. Is the grammar correct?

items.sentence: They aren't in the same country.
Is she your aunt? — Yes, she is.

steps.instruction: 45 seconds! Build each sentence by tapping the words in order.

steps.audio_text: Ray: Class, today is review day. Linh, tell us all about you. Linh: Hi everyone! I am Linh. I am nine. I was born in Vietnam. I live at 14 Le Loi Street in Ho Chi Minh City. My brother is called Minh. He is seven. My best friend is called Mai. My mum is a doctor and my dad is a teacher. My aunt Lan and my uncle Quan live near us. My grandparents live in a small town near a river. Ray: Lovely! And Tom, are you Vietnamese? Tom: No, I'm not. I am American. I am from a small town near New York. My friend Sophie is British. Lucas is Brazilian. We aren't in the same country, but we are in the same class on the internet. Ray: Class, you are amazing.

questions.question: Where does Linh's grandparents live?
questions.answer: in a small town near a river
options: in a small town near a river
questions.correct_answer: in a small town near a river

questions.question: Are the friends in the same country?

steps.prompt: Tell Ray all about you in 6-8 sentences. Use 'be' in different forms. Mention: name, age, country, nationality, your city / town / village, and 2 people in your family.
steps.expected_text: Hello Ray! I am [name]. I am [age] years old. I was born in [country]. I am [nationality]. I live in [city/town/village]. My [parent/aunt/uncle] is called [name]. My [brother/sister/best friend] is called [name]. We are happy.
prompts: Tell about 2 people in your family. Use 'is called'.
End with one positive 'we are' sentence.

steps.title: Unit 1 — All about me — Final check

questions.question: Choose: She ___ from Vietnam. (She's from the USA.)
Question form: 'He is your uncle.' → ___ he your uncle?
Lucas is from Brazil. He is ___.
Build: brother / is / My / Minh / called
A small place to live, smaller than a town, is a ___.
